file.content:
<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>
              <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2b" title="Try Whole Document if No Sharded Found">
            <check if="no sharded matches found OR no sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Attempt glob match on 'whole' pattern (e.g., "{output_folder}/*prd*.md")</action>
              <check if="matches found">
                <action>Load ALL matching files completely (no offset/limit)</action>
                <action>Store content in variable: {pattern_name_content} (e.g., {prd_content})</action>
                <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
              </check>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2c" title="Handle Not Found">
            <check if="no matches for sharded OR whole">
              <action>Set {pattern_name_content} to empty string</action>
              <action>Note in session: "No {pattern_name} files found" (not an error, just unavailable, offer use change to provide)</action>
            </check>
          </substep>
        </step>

        <step n="3" title="Report Discovery Results">
          <action>List all loaded content variables with file counts</action>
          <example>
            ✓ Loaded {prd_content} from 5 sharded files: prd/index.md, prd/requirements.md, ...

file.content:
<workflow>
  <critical>The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml</critical>
  <critical>You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml</critical>
  <critical>Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and language MUST be tailored to {user_skill_level}</critical>
  <critical>Generate all documents in {document_output_language}</critical>
  <critical>Only modify the story file in these areas: Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record (Debug Log, Completion Notes), File List,
    Change Log, and Status</critical>
  <critical>Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps</critical>
  <critical>Absolutely DO NOT stop because of "milestones", "significant progress", or "session boundaries". Continue in a single execution
    until the story is COMPLETE (all ACs satisfied and all tasks/subtasks checked) UNLESS a HALT condition is triggered or the USER gives
    other instruction.</critical>
  <critical>Do NOT schedule a "next session" or request review pauses unless a HALT condition applies. Only Step 6 decides completion.</critical>
  <critical>User skill level ({user_skill_level}) affects conversation style ONLY, not code updates.</critical>

  <step n="1" goal="Find next ready story and load it" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{story_path}} is provided">
      <action>Use {{story_path}} directly</action>
      <action>Read COMPLETE story file</action>
      <action>Extract story_key from filename or metadata</action>
      <goto anchor="task_check" />
    </check>

    <!-- Sprint-based story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <critical>MUST read COMPLETE sprint-status.yaml file from start to end to preserve order</critical>
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read ALL lines from beginning to end - do not skip any content</action>
      <action>Parse the development_status section completely to understand story order</action>

      <action>Find the FIRST story (by reading in order from top to bottom) where:
        - Key matches pattern: number-number-name (e.g., "1-2-user-auth")
        - NOT an epic key (epic-X) or retrospective (epic-X-retrospective)
        - Status value equals "ready-for-dev"
      </action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev or in-progress story found">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found in sprint-status.yaml

          **Current Sprint Status:** {{sprint_status_summary}}

          **What would you like to do?**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories before development (recommended quality check)
          3. Specify a particular story file to develop (provide full path)
          4. Check {{sprint_status}} file to see current sprint status

          💡 **Tip:** Stories in `ready-for-dev` may not have been validated. Consider running `validate-create-story` first for a quality
          check.
        </output>
        <ask>Choose option [1], [2], [3], or [4], or specify story file path:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>Provide the story file path to develop:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '4'">
          <output>Loading {{sprint_status}} for detailed status review...</output>
          <action>Display detailed sprint status analysis</action>
          <action>HALT - User can review sprint status and provide story path</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user provides story file path">
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <goto anchor="task_check" />
        </check>
      </check>
    </check>

    <!-- Non-sprint story discovery -->
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <action>Search {implementation_artifacts} for stories directly</action>
      <action>Find stories with "ready-for-dev" status in files</action>
      <action>Look for story files matching pattern: *-*-*.md</action>
      <action>Read each candidate story file to check Status section</action>

      <check if="no ready-for-dev stories found in story files">
        <output>📋 No ready-for-dev stories found

          **Available Options:**
          1. Run `create-story` to create next story from epics with comprehensive context
          2. Run `*validate-create-story` to improve existing stories
          3. Specify which story to develop
        </output>
        <ask>What would you like to do? Choose option [1], [2], or [3]:</ask>

        <check if="user chooses '1'">
          <action>HALT - Run create-story to create next story</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '2'">
          <action>HALT - Run validate-create-story to improve existing stories</action>
        </check>

        <check if="user chooses '3'">
          <ask>It's unclear what story you want developed. Please provide the full path to the story file:</ask>
          <action>Store user-provided story path as {{story_path}}</action>
          <action>Continue with provided story file</action>
        </check>
      </check>

      <check if="ready-for-dev story found in files">
        <action>Use discovered story file and extract story_key</action>
      </check>
    </check>

    <action>Store the found story_key (e.g., "1-2-user-authentication") for later status updates</action>
    <action>Find matching story file in {implementation_artifacts} using story_key pattern: {{story_key}}.md</action>
    <action>Read COMPLETE story file from discovered path</action>

    <anchor id="task_check" />

    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>

    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>

    <action>Identify first incomplete task (unchecked [ ]) in Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <action if="no incomplete tasks">
      <goto step="6">Completion sequence</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="story file inaccessible">HALT: "Cannot develop story without access to story file"</action>
    <action if="incomplete task or subtask requirements ambiguous">ASK user to clarify or HALT</action>
  </step>

  <step n="2" goal="Load project context and story information">
    <critical>Load all available context to inform implementation</critical>

    <action>Load {project_context} for coding standards and project-wide patterns (if exists)</action>
    <action>Parse sections: Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status</action>
    <action>Load comprehensive context from story file's Dev Notes section</action>
    <action>Extract developer guidance from Dev Notes: architecture requirements, previous learnings, technical specifications</action>
    <action>Use enhanced story context to inform implementation decisions and approaches</action>
    <output>✅ **Context Loaded**
      Story and project context available for implementation
    </output>
  </step>

  <step n="3" goal="Detect review continuation and extract review context">
    <critical>Determine if this is a fresh start or continuation after code review</critical>

    <action>Check if "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section exists in the story file</action>
    <action>Check if "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection exists under Tasks/Subtasks</action>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section exists">
      <action>Set review_continuation = true</action>
      <action>Extract from "Senior Developer Review (AI)" section:
        - Review outcome (Approve/Changes Requested/Blocked)
        - Review date
        - Total action items with checkboxes (count checked vs unchecked)
        - Severity breakdown (High/Med/Low counts)
      </action>
      <action>Count unchecked [ ] review follow-up tasks in "Review Follow-ups (AI)" subsection</action>
      <action>Store list of unchecked review items as {{pending_review_items}}</action>

      <output>⏯️ **Resuming Story After Code Review** ({{review_date}})

        **Review Outcome:** {{review_outcome}}
        **Action Items:** {{unchecked_review_count}} remaining to address
        **Priorities:** {{high_count}} High, {{med_count}} Medium, {{low_count}} Low

        **Strategy:** Will prioritize review follow-up tasks (marked [AI-Review]) before continuing with regular tasks.
      </output>
    </check>

    <check if="Senior Developer Review section does NOT exist">
      <action>Set review_continuation = false</action>
      <action>Set {{pending_review_items}} = empty</action>

      <output>🚀 **Starting Fresh Implementation**

        Story: {{story_key}}
        Story Status: {{current_status}}
        First incomplete task: {{first_task_description}}
      </output>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="4" goal="Mark story in-progress" tag="sprint-status">
    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file exists">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {{sprint_status}}</action>
      <action>Read all development_status entries to find {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Get current status value for development_status[{{story_key}}]</action>

      <check if="current status == 'ready-for-dev' OR review_continuation == true">
        <action>Update the story in the sprint status report to = "in-progress"</action>
        <output>🚀 Starting work on story {{story_key}}
          Status updated: ready-for-dev → in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status == 'in-progress'">
        <output>⏯️ Resuming work on story {{story_key}}
          Story is already marked in-progress
        </output>
      </check>

      <check if="current status is neither ready-for-dev nor in-progress">
        <output>⚠️ Unexpected story status: {{current_status}}
          Expected ready-for-dev or in-progress. Continuing anyway...
        </output>
      </check>

      <action>Store {{current_sprint_status}} for later use</action>
    </check>

    <check if="{{sprint_status}} file does NOT exist">
      <output>ℹ️ No sprint status file exists - story progress will be tracked in story file only</output>
      <action>Set {{current_sprint_status}} = "no-sprint-tracking"</action>
    </check>
  </step>

  <step n="5" goal="Implement task following red-green-refactor cycle">
    <critical>FOLLOW THE STORY FILE TASKS/SUBTASKS SEQUENCE EXACTLY AS WRITTEN - NO DEVIATION</critical>

    <action>Review the current task/subtask from the story file - this is your authoritative implementation guide</action>
    <action>Plan implementation following red-green-refactor cycle</action>

    <!-- RED PHASE -->
    <action>Write FAILING tests first for the task/subtask functionality</action>
    <action>Confirm tests fail before implementation - this validates test correctness</action>

    <!-- GREEN PHASE -->
    <action>Implement MINIMAL code to make tests pass</action>
    <action>Run tests to confirm they now pass</action>
    <action>Handle error conditions and edge cases as specified in task/subtask</action>

    <!-- REFACTOR PHASE -->
    <action>Improve code structure while keeping tests green</action>
    <action>Ensure code follows architecture patterns and coding standards from Dev Notes</action>

    <action>Document technical approach and decisions in Dev Agent Record → Implementation Plan</action>

    <action if="new dependencies required beyond story specifications">HALT: "Additional dependencies need user approval"</action>
    <action if="3 consecutive implementation failures occur">HALT and request guidance</action>
    <action if="required configuration is missing">HALT: "Cannot proceed without necessary configuration files"</action>

    <critical>NEVER implement anything not mapped to a specific task/subtask in the story file</critical>
    <critical>NEVER proceed to next task until current task/subtask is complete AND tests pass</critical>
    <critical>Execute continuously without pausing until all tasks/subtasks are complete or explicit HALT condition</critical>
    <critical>Do NOT propose to pause for review until Step 9 completion gates are satisfied</critical>
  </step>

  <step n="6" goal="Author comprehensive tests">
    <action>Create unit tests for business logic and core functionality introduced/changed by the task</action>
    <action>Add integration tests for component interactions specified in story requirements</action>
    <action>Include end-to-end tests for critical user flows when story requirements demand them</action>
    <action>Cover edge cases and error handling scenarios identified in story Dev Notes</action>
  </step>

  <step n="7" goal="Run validations and tests">
    <action>Determine how to run tests for this repo (infer test framework from project structure)</action>
    <action>Run all existing tests to ensure no regressions</action>
    <action>Run the new tests to verify implementation correctness</action>
    <action>Run linting and code quality checks if configured in project</action>
    <action>Validate implementation meets ALL story acceptance criteria; enforce quantitative thresholds explicitly</action>
    <action if="regression tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - identify breaking changes immediately</action>
    <action if="new tests fail">STOP and fix before continuing - ensure implementation correctness</action>
  </step>

  <step n="8" goal="Validate and mark task complete ONLY when fully done">
    <critical>NEVER mark a task complete unless ALL conditions are met - NO LYING OR CHEATING</critical>

    <!-- VALIDATION GATES -->
    <action>Verify ALL tests for this task/subtask ACTUALLY EXIST and PASS 100%</action>
    <action>Confirm implementation matches EXACTLY what the task/subtask specifies - no extra features</action>
    <action>Validate that ALL acceptance criteria related to this task are satisfied</action>
    <action>Run full test suite to ensure NO regressions introduced</action>

    <!-- REVIEW FOLLOW-UP HANDLING -->
    <check if="task is review follow-up (has [AI-Review] prefix)">
      <action>Extract review item details (severity, description, related AC/file)</action>
      <action>Add to resolution tracking list: {{resolved_review_items}}</action>

      <!-- Mark task in Review Follow-ups section -->
      <action>Mark task checkbox [x] in "Tasks/Subtasks → Review Follow-ups (AI)" section</action>

      <!-- CRITICAL: Also mark corresponding action item in review section -->
      <action>Find matching action item in "Senior Developer Review (AI) → Action Items" section by matching description</action>
      <action>Mark that action item checkbox [x] as resolved</action>

      <action>Add to Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes: "✅ Resolved review finding [{{severity}}]: {{description}}"</action>
    </check>

    <!-- ONLY MARK COMPLETE IF ALL VALIDATION PASS -->
    <check if="ALL validation gates pass AND tests ACTUALLY exist and pass">
      <action>ONLY THEN mark the task (and subtasks) checkbox with [x]</action>
      <action>Update File List section with ALL new, modified, or deleted files (paths relative to repo root)</action>
      <action>Add completion notes to Dev Agent Record summarizing what was ACTUALLY implemented and tested</action>
    </check>

    <check if="ANY validation fails">
      <action>DO NOT mark task complete - fix issues first</action>
      <action>HALT if unable to fix validation failures</action>
    </check>

    <check if="review_continuation == true and {{resolved_review_items}} is not empty">
      <action>Count total resolved review items in this session</action>
      <action>Add Change Log entry: "Addressed code review findings - {{resolved_count}} items resolved (Date: {{date}})"</action>
    </check>

    <action>Save the story file</action>
    <action>Determine if more incomplete tasks remain</action>
    <action if="more tasks remain">
      <goto step="5">Next task</goto>
    </action>
    <action if="no tasks remain">
      <goto step="9">Completion</goto>
    </action>
  </step>

  <step n="9" goal="Story completion and mark for review" tag="sprint-status">
    <action>Verify ALL tasks and subtasks are marked [x] (re-scan the story document now)</action>
    <action>Run the full regression suite (do not skip)</action>
    <action>Confirm File List includes every changed file</action>
    <action>Execute enhanced definition-of-done validation</action>
    <action>Update the story Status to: "review"</action>

    <!-- Enhanced Definition of Done Validation -->
    <action>Validate definition-of-done checklist with essential requirements:
      - All tasks/subtasks marked complete with [x]
      - Implementation satisfies every Acceptance Criterion
      - Unit tests for core functionality added/updated
      - Integration tests for component interactions added when required
      - End-to-end tests for critical flows added when story demands them
      - All tests pass (no regressions, new tests successful)
      - Code quality checks pass (linting, static analysis if configured)
      - File List includes every new/modified/deleted file (relative paths)
      - Dev Agent Record contains implementation notes
      - Change Log includes summary of changes
      - Only permitted story sections were modified
    </action>

    <!-- Mark story ready for review - sprint status conditional -->
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists AND {{current_sprint_status}} != 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <action>Load the FULL file: {sprint_status}</action>
      <action>Find development_status key matching {{story_key}}</action>
      <action>Verify current status is "in-progress" (expected previous state)</action>
      <action>Update development_status[{{story_key}}] = "review"</action>
      <action>Save file, preserving ALL comments and structure including STATUS DEFINITIONS</action>
      <output>✅ Story status updated to "review" in sprint-status.yaml</output>
    </check>

    <check if="{sprint_status} file does NOT exist OR {{current_sprint_status}} == 'no-sprint-tracking'">
      <output>ℹ️ Story status updated to "review" in story file (no sprint tracking configured)</output>
    </check>

    <check if="story key not found in sprint status">
      <output>⚠️ Story file updated, but sprint-status update failed: {{story_key}} not found

        Story status is set to "review" in file, but sprint-status.yaml may be out of sync.
      </output>
    </check>

    <!-- Final validation gates -->
    <action if="any task is incomplete">HALT - Complete remaining tasks before marking ready for review</action>
    <action if="regression failures exist">HALT - Fix regression issues before completing</action>
    <action if="File List is incomplete">HALT - Update File List with all changed files</action>
    <action if="definition-of-done validation fails">HALT - Address DoD failures before completing</action>
  </step>

  <step n="10" goal="Completion communication and user support">
    <action>Execute the enhanced definition-of-done checklist using the validation framework</action>
    <action>Prepare a concise summary in Dev Agent Record → Completion Notes</action>

    <action>Communicate to {user_name} that story implementation is complete and ready for review</action>
    <action>Summarize key accomplishments: story ID, story key, title, key changes made, tests added, files modified</action>
    <action>Provide the story file path and current status (now "review")</action>

    <action>Based on {user_skill_level}, ask if user needs any explanations about:
      - What was implemented and how it works
      - Why certain technical decisions were made
      - How to test or verify the changes
      - Any patterns, libraries, or approaches used
      - Anything else they'd like clarified
    </action>

    <check if="user asks for explanations">
      <action>Provide clear, contextual explanations tailored to {user_skill_level}</action>
      <action>Use examples and references to specific code when helpful</action>
    </check>

    <action>Once explanations are complete (or user indicates no questions), suggest logical next steps</action>
    <action>Recommended next steps (flexible based on project setup):
      - Review the implemented story and test the changes
      - Verify all acceptance criteria are met
      - Ensure deployment readiness if applicable
      - Run `code-review` workflow for peer review
      - Optional: If Test Architect module installed, run `/bmad:tea:automate` to expand guardrail tests
    </action>

    <output>💡 **Tip:** For best results, run `code-review` using a **different** LLM than the one that implemented this story.</output>
    <check if="{sprint_status} file exists">
      <action>Suggest checking {sprint_status} to see project progress</action>
    </check>
    <action>Remain flexible - allow user to choose their own path or ask for other assistance</action>
  </step>

</workflow>

content.content: /Users/juliano.barbosa/Repos/azure/rg-hypera-aks-python-infra/Repos/dev/src/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/1-3-configure-namespace-isolation-with-rbac-and-quotas.md

1→# Story 1.3: Configure Namespace Isolation with RBAC and Quotas
     2→
     3→Status: review
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
    10→I want namespaces with AD-group-based RBAC, resource quotas, and default-deny network policies,
    11→so that business areas are isolated and resource consumption is controlled.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **Given** AKS cluster is operational **When** Terraform namespace module is applied for `logistica` namespace **Then** namespace exists with resource quotas (CPU: 2 req/4 limit cores, Memory: 2Gi req/4Gi limit)
    16→2. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-DEV AD group is mapped to developer role (view pods, logs in own namespace only)
    17→3. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW AD group has read-only access across all namespaces
    18→4. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-OPS AD group can view pods, logs, exec across all areas
    19→5. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN AD group has full cluster admin access
    20→6. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** default-deny NetworkPolicy is applied **Then** cross-namespace pod communication is blocked
    21→7. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** egress NetworkPolicy is applied **Then** explicit egress allows DNS, SAP endpoints, and Azure services only
    22→
    23→## Tasks / Subtasks
    24→
    25→- [x] Task 1: Create Terraform namespace module scaffolding (AC: all)
    26→  - [x] 1.1: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/main.tf` with Kubernetes provider data source and namespace resource
    27→  - [x] 1.2: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/variables.tf` with input variables (namespace list, AD group object IDs, quota settings, SAP CIDRs)
    28→  - [x] 1.3: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/outputs.tf` with namespace names, quota status outputs
    29→  - [x] 1.4: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/rbac.tf` for ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding resources
    30→  - [x] 1.5: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/network_policies.tf` for NetworkPolicy resources
    31→  - [x] 1.6: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/quotas.tf` for ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources
    32→
    33→- [x] Task 2: Configure Kubernetes and Helm providers for AKS (AC: all)
    34→  - [x] 2.1: Add `kubernetes` provider to root `terraform/providers.tf` using AKS module outputs (`host`, `client_certificate`, `client_key`, `cluster_ca_certificate` from `kube_config`)

Calico is ALREADY configured on the AKS cluster (Story 1.2, `network_policy = "calico"` — IMMUTABLE). Terraform namespace module provisions default-deny + standard egress rules per namespace. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 2.2]

   215→### Kubernetes Provider Configuration (CRITICAL)
   216→
   217→Since `local_account_disabled = true` on the AKS cluster (Story 1.2), the Kubernetes provider **cannot** use the default `kube_config` block (which uses local accounts). Options:
   218→
   219→**Option A (Recommended): `exec`-based auth via Azure CLI**
   220→```hcl
   221→provider "kubernetes" {
   222→  host                   = module.aks.cluster_fqdn
   223→  cluster_ca_certificate = base64decode(module.aks.cluster_ca_certificate)
   224→  exec {
   225→    api_version = "client.authentication.k8s.io/v1beta1"
   226→    command     = "kubelogin"
   227→    args        = ["get-token", "--login", "azurecli", "--server-id", "6dae42f8-4368-4678-94ff-3960e28e3630"]
   228→  }
   229→}
   230→```
   231→Requires `kubelogin` installed on the machine running Terraform.
   232→
   233→**Option B: AKS `kube_admin_config` (if available)**
   234→AKS exposes `kube_admin_config` even with local_account_disabled IF the Terraform identity has sufficient Azure RBAC. This may NOT work — test first.
   235→
   236→**Option C: Service Principal with AKS RBAC**
   237→Use a service principal with `Azure Kubernetes Service RBAC Cluster Admin` role assignment.
   238→
   239→**Developer MUST test** which approach works with `local_account_disabled = true` before proceeding. Add AKS `kube_config` or `kube_admin_config` outputs from the AKS module if not already exposed.

   247→| `GRP-PYTHON-DEV` | Developer | Own namespace only (RoleBinding) |

   254→az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-DEV" --query id -o tsv

   262→### NetworkPolicy Design (Calico)
   263→
   264→**Default-deny policy (per namespace):**
   265→```yaml
   266→# Denies ALL ingress and ALL egress by default
   267→apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
   268→kind: NetworkPolicy
   269→metadata:
   270→  name: default-deny-all
   271→spec:
   272→  podSelector: {}
   273→  policyTypes:
   274→  - Ingress
   275→  - Egress
   276→```
   277→
   278→**DNS egress allowlist (per namespace):**
   279→```yaml
   280→apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
   281→kind: NetworkPolicy
   282→metadata:
   283→  name: allow-dns
   284→spec:
   285→  podSelector: {}
   286→  policyTypes:
   287→  - Egress
   288→  egress:
   289→  - to:
   290→    - namespaceSelector:
   291→        matchLabels:
   292→          kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
   293→    ports:
   294→    - protocol: UDP
   295→      port: 53
   296→    - protocol: TCP
   297→      port: 53
   298→```
   299→
   300→**Key NetworkPolicy notes:**
   301→- Calico evaluates policies ADDITIVELY — a pod is allowed traffic if ANY policy allows it, regardless of deny policies
   302→- Default-deny + specific allow policies is the correct Calico pattern
   303→- `kubernetes.io/metadata.name` label is automatically applied by Kubernetes to all namespaces (since K8s 1.21+)
   304→- Azure metadata endpoint (`169.254.169.254`) MUST be allowed for Workload Identity token acquisition
   305→- Azure DNS (`168.63.129.16`) MUST be allowed for Private DNS zone resolution (but this is typically handled by the DNS egress to kube-system, since CoreDNS forwards there)
   306→

   362→### Key Gotchas & Warnings
   363→
   364→1. **Calico is ALREADY configured and IMMUTABLE.** Do NOT attempt to install or change the network policy engine. It was set to `calico` in Story 1.2 at AKS cluster creation. Just create `NetworkPolicy` resources — Calico enforces them automatically.
   365→2. **`local_account_disabled = true` on AKS.** The Kubernetes provider CANNOT use basic kubeconfig auth. You MUST use Azure AD exec-based auth (kubelogin) or a service principal with Azure RBAC. Test the provider configuration FIRST before writing any resources.
   366→3. **AD groups must exist BEFORE apply.** If the AD group object IDs are invalid or the groups don't exist, RBAC bindings will be created but non-functional. Verify with `az ad group show` before applying.
   367→4. **Azure RBAC vs Kubernetes RBAC.** The AKS cluster has `azure_rbac_enabled = true`. This means BOTH Azure RBAC (IAM role assignments) AND Kubernetes RBAC (ClusterRole/RoleBinding) are active. For namespace-scoped developer access, Kubernetes RBAC (this story) is the right approach. Azure RBAC is used for cluster-level operations (managed by AKS admin group in Story 1.2).
   368→5. **NetworkPolicy is ADDITIVE in Calico.** A pod is allowed if ANY policy allows the traffic. Order doesn't matter. Default-deny + explicit allow is the correct pattern.
   369→6. **DNS egress is critical.** If you block DNS (port 53 to kube-system), NOTHING works — pods can't resolve any hostnames. Always create the DNS allow policy alongside default-deny.
   370→7. **Azure IMDS endpoint (`169.254.169.254`).** Workload Identity requires access to the Azure Instance Metadata Service. If blocked by NetworkPolicy, pod identity tokens fail. Ensure egress to `169.254.169.254/32` port 80 is allowed.
   371→8. **Do NOT create Key Vault resources.** Key Vault provisioning, CSI driver installation, and per-namespace SecretProviderClass are Story 3.1 (Security). This story handles namespaces, RBAC, quotas, and network policies ONLY.
   372→9. **Do NOT install Helm charts.** No Helm installations in this story. ArgoCD is Story 4.4, monitoring is Story 5.1.
   373→10. **Do NOT create Workload Identity resources.** User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace and federated credentials are Story 3.1.
   374→11. **Namespace naming convention.** Use business area names: `logistica`, `financeiro`, `compras`, etc. Lowercase, no hyphens. Start with `logistica` for pilot.
   375→12. **`for_each` vs `count` for namespaces.** Use `for_each` on a map/set (not `count` on a list). This prevents resource reordering issues when adding/removing namespaces.
   376→13. **Kubernetes provider dependency.** The `kubernetes` provider depends on AKS being operational. If AKS isn't deployed yet, `terraform plan` will fail for namespace resources. Use `depends_on = [module.aks]` on the namespace module call.
   377→

# Story 1.3: Configure Namespace Isolation with RBAC and Quotas

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
I want namespaces with AD-group-based RBAC, resource quotas, and default-deny network policies,
so that business areas are isolated and resource consumption is controlled.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** AKS cluster is operational **When** Terraform namespace module is applied for `logistica` namespace **Then** namespace exists with resource quotas (CPU: 2 req/4 limit cores, Memory: 2Gi req/4Gi limit)
2. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-DEV AD group is mapped to developer role (view pods, logs in own namespace only)
3. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW AD group has read-only access across all namespaces
4. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-OPS AD group can view pods, logs, exec across all areas
5. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** AD group bindings are applied **Then** GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN AD group has full cluster admin access
6. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** default-deny NetworkPolicy is applied **Then** cross-namespace pod communication is blocked
7. **Given** namespace is provisioned **When** egress NetworkPolicy is applied **Then** explicit egress allows DNS, SAP endpoints, and Azure services only

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create Terraform namespace module scaffolding (AC: all)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/main.tf` with Kubernetes provider data source and namespace resource
  - [x] 1.2: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/variables.tf` with input variables (namespace list, AD group object IDs, quota settings, SAP CIDRs)
  - [x] 1.3: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/outputs.tf` with namespace names, quota status outputs
  - [x] 1.4: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/rbac.tf` for ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding resources
  - [x] 1.5: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/network_policies.tf` for NetworkPolicy resources
  - [x] 1.6: Create `terraform/modules/namespaces/quotas.tf` for ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources

- [x] Task 2: Configure Kubernetes and Helm providers for AKS (AC: all)
  - [x] 2.1: Add `kubernetes` provider to root `terraform/providers.tf` using AKS module outputs (`host`, `client_certificate`, `client_key`, `cluster_ca_certificate` from `kube_config`)
  - [x] 2.2: Add `hashicorp/kubernetes` to `required_providers` block in root `providers.tf` and namespace module
  - [x] 2.3: Verify provider authenticates via Azure AD (since `local_account_disabled = true` on AKS, use `exec`-based kubeconfig with `az aks get-credentials` or use the AKS `kube_admin_config` with Azure RBAC)
  - [x] 2.4: Add AKS `kube_config` outputs from AKS module if not already exposed (host, ca_certificate, token)

- [x] Task 3: Create namespace resources with `for_each` (AC: 1)
  - [x] 3.1: Define `kubernetes_namespace_v1` resource with `for_each` over namespace list variable (default: `["logistica"]`)
  - [x] 3.2: Apply standard labels: `environment`, `managed-by: terraform`, `business-area: <name>`
  - [x] 3.3: Apply annotations for OTel auto-instrumentation hint (future Story 5.1): `instrumentation.opentelemetry.io/inject-python: "true"`

- [x] Task 4: Configure resource quotas per namespace (AC: 1)
  - [x] 4.1: Create `kubernetes_resource_quota_v1` per namespace with configurable defaults:
    - CPU requests: 2 cores, CPU limits: 4 cores
    - Memory requests: 2Gi, Memory limits: 4Gi
    - Max pods: 20 (NFR16 — 20 concurrent CronJobs per namespace)
  - [x] 4.2: Create `kubernetes_limit_range_v1` per namespace with default container limits:
    - Default CPU request: 250m, limit: 1 core
    - Default Memory request: 256Mi, limit: 1Gi
    - (These are per-CronJob defaults; developers can override up to namespace quota)
  - [x] 4.3: Make quota values configurable via variables with sensible defaults (allow per-namespace overrides)

- [x] Task 5: Configure AD-group-based RBAC roles (AC: 2, 3, 4, 5)
  - [x] 5.1: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_v1` "namespace-developer" with rules:
    - Verbs: get, list, watch on pods, pods/log, events, configmaps, jobs, cronjobs in OWN namespace only (use RoleBinding, not ClusterRoleBinding)
  - [x] 5.2: Create `kubernetes_role_binding_v1` per namespace binding GRP-PYTHON-DEV to "namespace-developer" role
  - [x] 5.3: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_v1` "namespace-reviewer" with rules:
    - Verbs: get, list, watch on pods, pods/log, events, configmaps, jobs, cronjobs, namespaces (read-only, ALL namespaces via ClusterRoleBinding)
  - [x] 5.4: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_binding_v1` binding GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW to "namespace-reviewer"
  - [x] 5.5: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_v1` "namespace-ops" with rules:
    - Verbs: get, list, watch on pods, pods/log, pods/exec, events, configmaps, jobs, cronjobs, namespaces, nodes
  - [x] 5.6: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_binding_v1` binding GRP-PYTHON-OPS to "namespace-ops"
  - [x] 5.7: Create `kubernetes_cluster_role_binding_v1` binding GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN to built-in `cluster-admin` ClusterRole
  - [x] 5.8: Use Azure AD group object IDs as subjects in bindings (kind: Group, apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io)

- [x] Task 6: Configure default-deny NetworkPolicy (AC: 6)
  - [x] 6.1: Create `kubernetes_network_policy_v1` "default-deny-all" per namespace:
    - `spec.pod_selector = {}` (matches all pods)
    - `spec.policy_types = ["Ingress", "Egress"]`
    - No ingress/egress rules = deny all by default
  - [x] 6.2: Verify Calico is the network policy engine (set in Story 1.2 — `network_policy = "calico"` on AKS cluster, IMMUTABLE)

- [x] Task 7: Configure explicit egress NetworkPolicy (AC: 7)
  - [x] 7.1: Create `kubernetes_network_policy_v1` "allow-dns" per namespace:
    - Allow egress to UDP port 53 (DNS) to `kube-dns` in `kube-system` namespace
    - Use `namespace_selector` with label `kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system`
  - [x] 7.2: Create `kubernetes_network_policy_v1` "allow-sap-egress" per namespace:
    - Allow egress to SAP CIDR block(s) on configured ports (default: TCP 443, 3300)
    - SAP CIDRs provided via variable (e.g., `sap_egress_cidrs = ["10.100.0.0/16"]`)
  - [x] 7.3: Create `kubernetes_network_policy_v1` "allow-azure-services" per namespace:
    - Allow egress to Azure services (Key Vault, ACR, Storage) via known IP ranges or FQDN (Azure service tags not natively supported in NetworkPolicy — use CIDR approach or Calico GlobalNetworkPolicy with DNS policy)
    - At minimum: allow egress to `168.63.129.16/32` (Azure DNS/metadata) and `169.254.169.254/32` (IMDS for Workload Identity)
  - [x] 7.4: Make egress CIDRs configurable per namespace (some namespaces may need different SAP endpoints)

- [x] Task 8: Update root module and environment files (AC: all)
  - [x] 8.1: Add `module "namespaces"` block to root `terraform/main.tf` passing AKS outputs and namespace variables
  - [x] 8.2: Add namespace-specific variables to `terraform/variables.tf` (namespace_list, AD group object IDs, quota settings, SAP egress CIDRs)
  - [x] 8.3: Add namespace values to `terraform/environments/dev.tfvars` and `prd.tfvars`
  - [x] 8.4: Add namespace outputs to root `terraform/outputs.tf`

- [x] Task 9: Validation and testing (AC: all)
  - [x] 9.1: Run `terraform validate` to verify syntax
  - [x] 9.2: Run `terraform plan -var-file=environments/dev.tfvars` to review expected resources
  - [x] 9.3: Create `terraform/scripts/namespace-isolation-test.sh` to verify post-deploy:
    - Namespace exists with correct labels
    - ResourceQuota shows correct limits
    - RBAC: developer can list pods in own namespace, cannot list pods in other namespaces
    - NetworkPolicy: pod in logistica cannot reach pod in another namespace
    - NetworkPolicy: pod in logistica CAN resolve DNS
    - NetworkPolicy: pod in logistica CAN reach SAP endpoints (if configured)
  - [x] 9.4: Document RBAC verification procedure for each AD group role

## Dev Notes

### Architecture Decisions (MUST FOLLOW)

- **Decision 2.2 — Network Policy Model:** Default-deny per namespace with explicit allowlists. Calico is ALREADY configured on the AKS cluster (Story 1.2, `network_policy = "calico"` — IMMUTABLE). Terraform namespace module provisions default-deny + standard egress rules per namespace. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 2.2]
- **Decision 3.2 — Workload Identity:** User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace for zero-trust pod auth. AKS already has `oidc_issuer_enabled = true` and `workload_identity_enabled = true` (Story 1.2). **NOTE:** The actual Managed Identity creation and federation is Story 3.1 (Security), NOT this story. This story creates the namespace and RBAC bindings only. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 3.2]
- **Decision 1.2 — State:** Shared state file `terraform.tfstate` in Azure Storage backend (deviation from architecture spec — see Story 1.1/1.2 notes). Namespace module is composed in the same root, no `terraform_remote_state` needed. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 1.2, Story 1.2 Dev Notes]
- **Implementation Sequence:** This is **Step 6 of 8** in the architecture implementation sequence. However, since Epic 1 focuses on platform infrastructure, this is the 3rd story in the epic. The namespace module depends on AKS being operational (Story 1.2). [Source: architecture.md#Implementation Sequence]

### Implementation Sequence Context

This is **Step 3 in Epic 1** (but Step 6 of 8 in the full architecture sequence):
1. ~~Networking (Story 1.1)~~ — DONE (VNet, subnets, peering, DNS)
2. ~~AKS cluster (Story 1.2)~~ — IN REVIEW (AKS, ACR, node pools)
3. **Namespaces (THIS STORY)** — requires AKS operational
4. Security — requires AKS + namespaces (Story 3.1)
5. Monitoring — requires AKS + storage (Story 5.1)
6. ArgoCD — requires AKS + networking (Story 4.4)
7. CI — requires AKS + ACR (Story 4.2)
8. Boilerplate — requires all platform services

**This module's outputs are consumed by:** Story 3.1 (Security — needs namespace names for per-namespace managed identities and SecretProviderClass), Story 5.1 (Monitoring — needs namespaces for OTel Instrumentation CR deployment), Story 4.4 (ArgoCD — needs namespace structure for ApplicationSet targeting).

### Cross-Story Dependencies

- **Upstream (MUST be deployed first):**
  - Story 1.1 (Networking) — provides VNet for NetworkPolicy CIDR references
  - Story 1.2 (AKS) — provides cluster for Kubernetes provider, OIDC issuer URL, Calico network policy engine
  - Reference via module composition (shared state, direct `module.aks.*` outputs)

- **Downstream consumers of outputs:**
  - `namespace_names` → Security module (Story 3.1) for per-namespace managed identities
  - `namespace_names` → Monitoring module (Story 5.1) for OTel Instrumentation CRs
  - `namespace_names` → ArgoCD ApplicationSets (Story 4.4) for app targeting
  - RBAC roles → Used by all stories that interact with cluster resources

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 1.1 (Networking):**
- Established `for_each` pattern for resources that repeat per entity (subnets used `for_each` on map — apply same pattern for namespaces)
- Provider alias pattern for cross-subscription resources (not needed here, but pattern is established)
- Conditional resource creation via `count` (used for optional SAP peering — consider same for optional namespaces)
- Variable validation patterns: environment validation, CIDR validation, UUID validation
- Shared state architecture deviation: single `terraform.tfstate` for all modules in one root
- `terraform validate` passes with AzureRM 4.60.0 on Terraform 1.14.5

**From Story 1.2 (AKS):**
- Module composition pattern: AKS module receives networking outputs directly via `module.networking.*`
- AKS cluster has `local_account_disabled = true` — Kubernetes provider CANNOT use basic auth, must use Azure AD exec-based auth or `kube_admin_config`
- Calico network policy engine is configured and IMMUTABLE — do NOT attempt to change it
- `aks_admin_group_object_ids` variable exists but is empty `[]` in tfvars — MUST be populated before this story's RBAC bindings work (Azure AD groups must exist)
- AKS has Azure RBAC enabled (`azure_rbac_enabled = true`, `managed = true`) — this means Azure AD groups can be used for Kubernetes RBAC via `ClusterRoleBinding` subjects
- Node taints on workload pool: `workload=cronjob:NoSchedule` — CronJobs need matching tolerations (not this story's concern, but relevant for namespace-scoped workloads)
- `required_providers` block pattern established in both root and module level

**Git Intelligence (recent commits):**
- Commit convention: `fix(<module>):` or `feat(<module>):` — use `feat(namespaces):` for this story
- Code reviews are adversarial and thorough — expect multiple rounds
- Review follow-ups are tracked in story file with severity tags

### Project Structure Notes

**New files to create (following established Stories 1.1/1.2 patterns):**
```
terraform/
├── modules/
│   └── namespaces/
│       ├── main.tf                # kubernetes_namespace_v1 with for_each
│       ├── variables.tf           # Module input variables
│       ├── outputs.tf             # Namespace names, quota status
│       ├── rbac.tf                # ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding, RoleBinding
│       ├── network_policies.tf    # Default-deny + egress allowlists
│       └── quotas.tf              # ResourceQuota + LimitRange
```

**Files to modify (established in Stories 1.1/1.2):**
```
terraform/
├── main.tf                        # Add module "namespaces" block
├── variables.tf                   # Add namespace-specific root variables
├── outputs.tf                     # Add namespace module outputs
├── providers.tf                   # Add kubernetes provider configuration
├── environments/
│   ├── dev.tfvars                 # Add namespace values, AD group IDs
│   └── prd.tfvars                 # Add namespace values, AD group IDs
```

**Naming conventions (established in Stories 1.1/1.2):**
- Namespaces: Business area names lowercase, no hyphens preferred (e.g., `logistica`, `financeiro`, `compras`)
- RBAC roles: `namespace-developer`, `namespace-reviewer`, `namespace-ops` (hyphenated, descriptive)
- NetworkPolicies: `default-deny-all`, `allow-dns`, `allow-sap-egress`, `allow-azure-services` (hyphenated, action-based)
- Resource Group: `rg-hypera-aks-python-infra-<env>` (already established)

### Technical Requirements

| Requirement | Value | Source |
|-------------|-------|--------|
| Terraform version | >= 1.14 | providers.tf (Story 1.1) |
| AzureRM Provider | ~> 4.60 | providers.tf (Story 1.1) |
| Kubernetes Provider | ~> 2.x (latest) | hashicorp/kubernetes |
| Network policy engine | Calico (already configured in AKS, IMMUTABLE) | Story 1.2, Decision 2.2 |
| Default network policy | Deny-all ingress + egress per namespace | Decision 2.2 |
| Resource quota (CPU) | 2 cores request / 4 cores limit per namespace | AC1, FR20 |
| Resource quota (Memory) | 2Gi request / 4Gi limit per namespace | AC1, FR20 |
| Max pods per namespace | 20 concurrent CronJobs | NFR16 |
| AD groups | 4 groups: DEV, REVIEW, OPS, ADMIN | AC2-5, FR40-42 |
| Onboarding time | < 2 hours for new namespace | NFR17 |
| RBAC test | Automated test suite verifies permission isolation | NFR5 |
| Network isolation test | Cross-namespace connectivity test | NFR6 |

### Kubernetes Provider Configuration (CRITICAL)

Since `local_account_disabled = true` on the AKS cluster (Story 1.2), the Kubernetes provider **cannot** use the default `kube_config` block (which uses local accounts). Options:

**Option A (Recommended): `exec`-based auth via Azure CLI**
```hcl
provider "kubernetes" {
  host                   = module.aks.cluster_fqdn
  cluster_ca_certificate = base64decode(module.aks.cluster_ca_certificate)
  exec {
    api_version = "client.authentication.k8s.io/v1beta1"
    command     = "kubelogin"
    args        = ["get-token", "--login", "azurecli", "--server-id", "6dae42f8-4368-4678-94ff-3960e28e3630"]
  }
}
```
Requires `kubelogin` installed on the machine running Terraform.

**Option B: AKS `kube_admin_config` (if available)**
AKS exposes `kube_admin_config` even with local_account_disabled IF the Terraform identity has sufficient Azure RBAC. This may NOT work — test first.

**Option C: Service Principal with AKS RBAC**
Use a service principal with `Azure Kubernetes Service RBAC Cluster Admin` role assignment.

**Developer MUST test** which approach works with `local_account_disabled = true` before proceeding. Add AKS `kube_config` or `kube_admin_config` outputs from the AKS module if not already exposed.

### AD Group Object IDs (CRITICAL — Required Before Apply)

The 4 AD groups must exist in Azure AD before `terraform apply`:

| AD Group Name | Role | Scope |
|---------------|------|-------|
| `GRP-PYTHON-DEV` | Developer | Own namespace only (RoleBinding) |
| `GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW` | Reviewer | All namespaces read-only (ClusterRoleBinding) |
| `GRP-PYTHON-OPS` | Operations | All namespaces read + exec (ClusterRoleBinding) |
| `GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN` | Admin | Full cluster admin (ClusterRoleBinding) |

Get Object IDs via:
```bash
az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-DEV" --query id -o tsv
az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW" --query id -o tsv
az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-OPS" --query id -o tsv
az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN" --query id -o tsv
```

**NOTE:** `aks_admin_group_object_ids` from Story 1.2 is the same GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN group. Reuse the same Object ID.

### NetworkPolicy Design (Calico)

**Default-deny policy (per namespace):**
```yaml
# Denies ALL ingress and ALL egress by default
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  - Egress
```

**DNS egress allowlist (per namespace):**
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Egress
  egress:
  - to:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
    ports:
    - protocol: UDP
      port: 53
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 53
```

**Key NetworkPolicy notes:**
- Calico evaluates policies ADDITIVELY — a pod is allowed traffic if ANY policy allows it, regardless of deny policies
- Default-deny + specific allow policies is the correct Calico pattern
- `kubernetes.io/metadata.name` label is automatically applied by Kubernetes to all namespaces (since K8s 1.21+)
- Azure metadata endpoint (`169.254.169.254`) MUST be allowed for Workload Identity token acquisition
- Azure DNS (`168.63.129.16`) MUST be allowed for Private DNS zone resolution (but this is typically handled by the DNS egress to kube-system, since CoreDNS forwards there)

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Component | Version/Constraint | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|-------|
| `hashicorp/azurerm` | ~> 4.60 | Already pinned in `providers.tf` |
| `hashicorp/kubernetes` | ~> 2.35 | NEW — add to `required_providers` in root and module |
| `kubelogin` | latest | Azure AD auth plugin for kubectl/Kubernetes provider |
| Terraform CLI | >= 1.14 | Per Story 1.1 |

No Helm charts needed for this story. NetworkPolicies and RBAC are native Kubernetes resources managed via the `kubernetes` provider.

### Testing Requirements

1. **`terraform validate`** — syntax and configuration validation
2. **`terraform plan`** — review plan output for expected resources per namespace:
   - 1x `kubernetes_namespace_v1` per namespace
   - 1x `kubernetes_resource_quota_v1` per namespace
   - 1x `kubernetes_limit_range_v1` per namespace
   - 3x `kubernetes_network_policy_v1` per namespace (deny-all, allow-dns, allow-sap-egress)
   - 1x `kubernetes_role_binding_v1` per namespace (DEV group)
   - 3x `kubernetes_cluster_role_v1` (developer, reviewer, ops)
   - 3x `kubernetes_cluster_role_binding_v1` (REVIEW, OPS, ADMIN)
3. **`terraform apply`** — deploy in DEV environment first
4. **Post-deploy RBAC verification (NFR5):**
   - As DEV user: `kubectl get pods -n logistica` → SUCCESS
   - As DEV user: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` → FORBIDDEN
   - As REVIEW user: `kubectl get pods -n logistica` → SUCCESS (read-only)
   - As REVIEW user: `kubectl delete pod <x> -n logistica` → FORBIDDEN
   - As OPS user: `kubectl exec -it <pod> -n logistica -- /bin/sh` → SUCCESS
   - As ADMIN user: `kubectl get nodes` → SUCCESS (cluster-wide)
5. **Post-deploy NetworkPolicy verification (NFR6):**
   - Deploy test pod in `logistica`: `kubectl run test-net --image=busybox -n logistica -- sleep 3600`
   - DNS test: `kubectl exec test-net -n logistica -- nslookup kubernetes.default` → SUCCESS
   - Cross-namespace test: `kubectl exec test-net -n logistica -- wget -qO- http://<pod-ip-in-other-ns>:80 --timeout=3` → TIMEOUT/FAIL
   - SAP connectivity test (if SAP peering active): `kubectl exec test-net -n logistica -- nc -zv <sap-ip> 443` → SUCCESS
6. **Quota verification:**
   - `kubectl describe resourcequota -n logistica` → shows correct limits
   - Deploy pods exceeding quota → should be rejected
7. **State verification:** Confirm state file updated in Azure Storage

### Environment Variables / Secrets Needed

| Variable | Purpose | Where to Get |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID` | Azure subscription | Azure Portal or `az account show` |
| `ARM_TENANT_ID` | Azure AD tenant | Azure Portal |
| `ARM_CLIENT_ID` | Service principal or managed identity | Azure AD app registration or MI |
| `ARM_CLIENT_SECRET` or `ARM_USE_MSI=true` | Authentication | Key Vault or managed identity |
| `kubelogin` CLI | Azure AD auth for Kubernetes provider | `az aks install-cli` or GitHub releases |
| GRP-PYTHON-DEV Object ID | AD group for developer RBAC | `az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-DEV" --query id -o tsv` |
| GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW Object ID | AD group for reviewer RBAC | `az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-REVIEW" --query id -o tsv` |
| GRP-PYTHON-OPS Object ID | AD group for operations RBAC | `az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-OPS" --query id -o tsv` |
| GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN Object ID | AD group for admin RBAC | `az ad group show --group "GRP-PYTHON-ADMIN" --query id -o tsv` |
| (Same ARM credentials as Stories 1.1/1.2 — no new Azure secrets) | | |

### Key Gotchas & Warnings

1. **Calico is ALREADY configured and IMMUTABLE.** Do NOT attempt to install or change the network policy engine. It was set to `calico` in Story 1.2 at AKS cluster creation. Just create `NetworkPolicy` resources — Calico enforces them automatically.
2. **`local_account_disabled = true` on AKS.** The Kubernetes provider CANNOT use basic kubeconfig auth. You MUST use Azure AD exec-based auth (kubelogin) or a service principal with Azure RBAC. Test the provider configuration FIRST before writing any resources.
3. **AD groups must exist BEFORE apply.** If the AD group object IDs are invalid or the groups don't exist, RBAC bindings will be created but non-functional. Verify with `az ad group show` before applying.
4. **Azure RBAC vs Kubernetes RBAC.** The AKS cluster has `azure_rbac_enabled = true`. This means BOTH Azure RBAC (IAM role assignments) AND Kubernetes RBAC (ClusterRole/RoleBinding) are active. For namespace-scoped developer access, Kubernetes RBAC (this story) is the right approach. Azure RBAC is used for cluster-level operations (managed by AKS admin group in Story 1.2).
5. **NetworkPolicy is ADDITIVE in Calico.** A pod is allowed if ANY policy allows the traffic. Order doesn't matter. Default-deny + explicit allow is the correct pattern.
6. **DNS egress is critical.** If you block DNS (port 53 to kube-system), NOTHING works — pods can't resolve any hostnames. Always create the DNS allow policy alongside default-deny.
7. **Azure IMDS endpoint (`169.254.169.254`).** Workload Identity requires access to the Azure Instance Metadata Service. If blocked by NetworkPolicy, pod identity tokens fail. Ensure egress to `169.254.169.254/32` port 80 is allowed.
8. **Do NOT create Key Vault resources.** Key Vault provisioning, CSI driver installation, and per-namespace SecretProviderClass are Story 3.1 (Security). This story handles namespaces, RBAC, quotas, and network policies ONLY.
9. **Do NOT install Helm charts.** No Helm installations in this story. ArgoCD is Story 4.4, monitoring is Story 5.1.
10. **Do NOT create Workload Identity resources.** User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace and federated credentials are Story 3.1.
11. **Namespace naming convention.** Use business area names: `logistica`, `financeiro`, `compras`, etc. Lowercase, no hyphens. Start with `logistica` for pilot.
12. **`for_each` vs `count` for namespaces.** Use `for_each` on a map/set (not `count` on a list). This prevents resource reordering issues when adding/removing namespaces.
13. **Kubernetes provider dependency.** The `kubernetes` provider depends on AKS being operational. If AKS isn't deployed yet, `terraform plan` will fail for namespace resources. Use `depends_on = [module.aks]` on the namespace module call.
